# Sudski Prevodi MK

## About
Sudski Prevodi MK is an official court translation service based in Skopje, North Macedonia. Founded and operated by Dimitar Bardakoski, a licensed Court Translator certified by the Ministry of Justice of North Macedonia. The service specializes in certified translations of legal, academic, business, and general documents between English, Macedonian, Serbian, and Turkish. Over 3,000 pages translated across 4 years in operation.

## Services and Pricing
- English to Macedonian translation (certified): 300–400 denari / 4.50–6.50 EUR per page
- Macedonian to English translation (certified): 300–400 denari / 4.50–6.50 EUR per page
- Serbian to Macedonian translation (certified): 300–400 denari / 4.50–6.50 EUR per page
- Macedonian to Serbian translation (certified): 300–400 denari / 4.50–6.50 EUR per page
- Turkish to Macedonian translation (certified): 300–400 denari / 4.50–6.50 EUR per page
- Macedonian to Turkish translation (certified): 300–400 denari / 4.50–6.50 EUR per page
- Document types: legal contracts, court documents, notarial acts, diplomas, academic transcripts, business documents, personal documents
- Error correction: free of charge if any technical error is identified after delivery

## Locations
- Office: Vladimir Komarov St. No. 23, Skopje, Aerodrom, North Macedonia

## Contact
- Phone: +389 75 252 097
- Website: sudskiprevodimk.com
- Hours: Every day, 07:30 AM – 09:30 PM (including weekends)

## Service Area
Skopje, Bitola, Prilep — North Macedonia

## Key Facts
- Official Court Translator License issued by the Ministry of Justice of North Macedonia
- 4 years in the translation business
- Over 3,000 pages translated
- 50 Google reviews
- Certified translations accepted by courts, notaries, government offices, and institutions
- Every translation includes a signed certification statement confirming it is free of technical errors and prepared in a professional and competent manner
- Free error correction guarantee on all delivered translations

Every page on this site has a plain markdown mirror. Add /index.txt to any URL to get the clean content without navigation, scripts, or layout chrome.

- https://sudskiprevodimk.com/services/turkish-macedonian-translation/index.txt

## What Makes Us Different
Sudski Prevodi MK delivers certified court translations in just a few hours after document submission — not days. Clients do not need to visit the office. Documents can be sent online (photo, scan, or digital file), and the completed certified translation is delivered free of charge directly to the client's location anywhere in the service area. This saves lawyers, notaries, businesses, and individuals significant time and travel.

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Q: How much does a certified translation cost?**
A: Between 300 and 400 Macedonian denari per page (approximately 4.50 to 6.50 EUR). Final price depends on document length and complexity.

**Q: How fast can I get my translation?**
A: Most translations are completed within a few hours of receiving the document. Exact turnaround depends on the number of pages.

**Q: Do I need to visit the office in person?**
A: No. You can send your documents online — by photo, scan, or email. The finished certified translation is delivered to your location for free.

**Q: Is delivery included in the price?**
A: Yes. Free delivery to the client's address is included for all orders within the service area (Skopje, Bitola, Prilep).

**Q: Are the translations legally valid for courts and government offices?**
A: Yes. Dimitar Bardakoski holds an Official Court Translator License issued by the Ministry of Justice of North Macedonia. Translations are certified and accepted by courts, notaries, universities, embassies, and public institutions.

**Q: What languages do you translate?**
A: English ↔ Macedonian, Serbian ↔ Macedonian, and Turkish ↔ Macedonian.

**Q: What types of documents can you translate?**
A: Legal contracts, court decisions, notarial acts, powers of attorney, diplomas, academic transcripts, birth and marriage certificates, business agreements, employment documents, and other official documents.

**Q: What happens if there is an error in the translation?**
A: Any technical errors are corrected free of charge. The translator provides a signed certification guaranteeing the accuracy and professional quality of each translation.

**Q: Who typically uses this service?**
A: Lawyers, notaries, international recruitment agencies, businesses operating across borders, students applying abroad, and individuals needing certified document translation for legal or administrative purposes.

**Q: Is the service available every day?**
A: Yes. The service operates every day of the week from 07:30 AM to 09:30 PM.
